decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.2°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since recent geometry adjustments have hit legality limits and stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest-priority lever to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the peak temperature.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution, lowering the local hot spot on the chip. This is the primary recommended lever when geometry changes are constrained.

expected_effects: Improve thermal uniformity across the heat spreader
risk_notes: The proposed value (1000.0) exceeds the documented upper bound of 500.0 for this variable; if strict bounds enforcement prevents this change, fallback to increasing base_material.conductivity or adjusting spreader dimensions within valid ranges.
